column1: 1|Complete the AWS Migration|Move all source data, team-owned processes, and Partner-agnostic alternative-data views onto AWS. Benefits: retires legacy Hadoop/SAS compute costs; unblocks analytical users locked out of direct Partner data; gives downstream AI a single platform to target.|objective 1 [P1] - Migrate source data (PCDS/Hadoop/SAS) to AWS\nobjective 3 [P1] - Migrate team-owned processes to AWS\nobjective 4 [P1] - Publish Partner-agnostic views for analytical users

2|Establish Single Source of Truth with Clear Ownership and Full Compliance|One authoritative version per critical dataset, unambiguous owners across build/validate/monitor/fix, and 100% GDMS/CDE coverage. Benefits: ends "whose number is right?" debates; issue routing takes hours not weeks because owners are named; passes enterprise/regulator audits first look.|objective 5 [P2] - One authoritative version per critical dataset\nobjective 8 [P2] - Assign clear owners across data lifecycle stages\nobjective 10 [P3] - Full GDMS compliance for all CDEs

3|Deliver Self-Service, High-Quality Data for Analysts|Active metadata in EDP Marketplace, consistent definitions and shapes across datasets, and preventive (not just detective) data-quality controls. Benefits: analyst time-to-data drops from days to minutes; defects blocked upstream instead of surfacing in reports; fewer re-pulls per analytical ask.|objective 9 [P2] - Make AWS data easy to discover and use\nobjective 11 [P3] - Active metadata catalog in EDP Marketplace\nobjective 12 [P3] - Shift data quality from detection to prevention

4|Unify Analytics and Validation Inside Databricks|Run validation, dashboards, and exception handling end-to-end in Databricks with a structured feedback loop to the platform team. Benefits: eliminates SAS-server -> AWS-CLI -> local round-trips per validation cycle; platform evolves around actual USCB use cases; single pane of glass for results.|objective 13 [P4] - Reduce Databricks workflow friction for analysts\nobjective 14 [P4] - End-to-end validation inside Databricks with dashboards

5|Embed AI Across Data Operations and Team Workflows|AI inside the platform (metadata enrichment, completeness monitoring, self-healing pipelines, auto-documented processes) and in team tooling (curated Copilot prompts with security guardrails for onboarding, metrics, decks). Benefits: data-ops toil drops materially; team reclaims hours on recurring deliverables while staying inside approved info boundaries.|objective 2 [P1] - Enable AI use via AWS data governance\nobjective 15 [P4] - Reusable Copilot prompts for data-strategy work
